summary: The diff implements all three core requirements: REQ-1 (remove caller/principal checks), REQ-2 (re-enable message deletion on admin page), and REQ-3 (per-user pinned videos). However, REQ-1 has a critical gap: the backend/main.mo still contains 'public shared ({ caller })' function signatures for initializeAccessControl and getCallerUserR, and imports/uses an AccessControl module, meaning caller references and principal-based access control have NOT been fully removed.

missing_requirements.requirement: Remove every caller/principal check from every function in backend/main.mo. No references to 'caller' should exist anywhere, no Principal-based access control checks, and all functions callable without identity restrictions.
missing_requirements.reason: The truncated backend/main.mo still shows 'public shared ({ caller }) func initializeAccessControl()' and 'public query ({ caller }) func getCallerUserR', and imports both Principal and an AccessControl module with 'let accessControlState = AccessControl.initState()'. These are explicit caller/principal references that violate the requirement.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) was added to handle stripping pinnedVideoId from the old Chatroom type during schema migration.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention adding a migration module. This is extra infrastructure not explicitly requested, though it may be a necessary side-effect of removing pinnedVideoId from the Chatroom struct.
